config.name: cisco_networks_logs-latest_transform
config.lua_code:
-- SentinelOne Parser: cisco_networks_logs-latest
-- OCSF Class: Infrastructure Events (4002)
-- Performance Level: High-volume optimized
-- Generated: 2025-10-13T11:03:24.865291

-- Pre-compile patterns for performance
local TIMESTAMP_PATTERN = "^(%d%d%d%d%-%d%d%-%d%d[T ]%d%d:%d%d:%d%d)"
local IP_PATTERN = "^%d+%.%d+%.%d+%.%d+$"

-- Cached references for performance
local type = type
local tonumber = tonumber
local os_time = os.time
local string_match = string.match
local string_format = string.format

function transform(record)
    -- Input validation with early return
    if not record or type(record) ~= "table" then
        return nil, "Invalid input record"
    end

    -- Initialize OCSF-compliant output with local table
    local output = {
        -- Required OCSF fields
        class_uid = 4002,
        class_name = "Infrastructure Events", 
        category_uid = 4,
        category_name = "Cisco IOS",
        metadata = {
            product = {
                name = "Cisco Networking",
                vendor_name = "Cisco"
            }
        },
        device = {},
        network = {},
        time = nil
    }

    -- Timestamp handling with validation
    local timestamp = record.timestamp
    if timestamp then
        local parsed_ts = string_match(timestamp, TIMESTAMP_PATTERN)
        if parsed_ts then
            -- Convert to UNIX milliseconds
            output.time = os_time({
                year = tonumber(string_match(parsed_ts, "(%d%d%d%d)")),
                month = tonumber(string_match(parsed_ts, "-%d%d-(%d%d)")),
                day = tonumber(string_match(parsed_ts, "-%d%d$")),
                hour = tonumber(string_match(parsed_ts, " (%d%d):")),
                min = tonumber(string_match(parsed_ts, ":(%d%d):")),
                sec = tonumber(string_match(parsed_ts, ":(%d%d)$"))
            }) * 1000
        end
    end

    -- Device name mapping with validation
    if record.device and type(record.device) == "string" then
        output.device.name = record.device
    end

    -- Event type mapping
    if record.event_type and type(record.event_type) == "string" then
        output.activity_name = record.event_type
        -- Map common event types to activity IDs
        if record.event_type:match("unauthorized") then
            output.activity_id = 1
        elseif record.event_type:match("VLAN") then
            output.activity_id = 2
        else
            output.activity_id = 0
        end
    end

    -- Protocol mapping with validation
    if record.protocol and type(record.protocol) == "string" then
        output.network.protocol = record.protocol:upper()
    end

    -- Handle special fields based on message type
    if record.nms_ip and string_match(record.nms_ip, IP_PATTERN) then
        output.src_endpoint = {
            ip = record.nms_ip
        }
    end

    -- Interface handling
    if record.interface then
        output.network.interface = {
            name = record.interface
        }
    end

    -- Route table specific fields
    if record.route_type then
        output.network.routing = {
            type = record.route_type,
            network = record.network,
            gateway = record.gateway,
            mask = record.mask
        }
    end

    -- Ensure required fields are present
    if not output.time then
        output.time = os_time() * 1000
    end

    -- Final validation
    if not output.activity_name then
        output.activity_name = "Unknown Activity"
    end

    return output
end

-- Error wrapper for production use
function safe_transform(record)
    local success, result = pcall(transform, record)
    if not success then
        return nil, string_format("Transform error: %s", result)
    end
    return result
end
